title: rosa palustris var. aculeata
intro: Rosa palustris var. aculeata, commonly known as swamp rose, is a prickly variant of the Rosa palustris species, a flowering shrub native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rosa palustris var. aculeata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
